revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=高屋神社| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市古市10| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|39|N|135|36|32|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=饒速...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=高屋神社| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市古市10| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|39|N|135|36|32|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=饒速日命
広国押武金日命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 創建=[[宣化天皇]]3年（[[538年]]）}} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]と広国押武金日命（[[安閑天皇]]）を祀る。当地は[[物部氏]]の系統で、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、[[祖神]]として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 社伝によれば、[[宣化天皇]]3年（[[538年]]）、勅命により創建された。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、明治初年に高屋神社に改称し、明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格した。明治41年（[[1904年]]）5月11日、白鳥神社（羽曳野市古市1-1）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。昭和29年（[[1964年]]）、高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040202-01.html 高屋神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/monofuru.htm 高屋神社]（神奈備） {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市]]

revision.comment: [[白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|]]
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=高屋神社| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市古市10| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|39|N|135|36|32|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=饒速日命
広国押武金日命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 創建=[[宣化天皇]]3年（[[538年]]）}} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]と広国押武金日命（[[安閑天皇]]）を祀る。当地は[[物部氏]]の系統で、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、[[祖神]]として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 社伝によれば、[[宣化天皇]]3年（[[538年]]）、勅命により創建された。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、明治初年に高屋神社に改称し、明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格した。明治41年（[[1904年]]）5月11日、[[白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|白鳥神社]]（羽曳野市古市1-1）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。昭和29年（[[1964年]]）、高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040202-01.html 高屋神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/monofuru.htm 高屋神社]（神奈備） {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市]]

旧[[村社]] |創建=[[宣化天皇]]3年（[[538年]]） |本殿=一間社流造 |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]と広国押武金日命（[[安閑天皇]]）を祀る。 当地は[[物部氏]]の系統で、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、[[祖神]]として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[538年]]（[[宣化天皇]]3年）、勅命により創建された。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、[[1868年]]（明治初年）に高屋神社に改称し、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列格した。[[1904年]]（明治41年）5月11日、[[白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|白鳥神社]]（羽曳野市古市1-1）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも2018年現在も高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ==境内== === 境内社 === *末社：宮守稲荷社（祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市6-12 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅]]下車後、徒歩約15分（南へ約1.2km） ==関連項目== *[[辛国神社]] - 藤井寺市にある式内社。高屋神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である辛国連の本貫地であった。 {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

[[安閑天皇|広国押武金日命]] |社格=[[式内小社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=[[宣化天皇]]3年（[[538年]]） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高屋神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]と広国押武金日命（[[安閑天皇]]）を祀る。 当地は[[物部氏]]の系統で、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、[[祖神]]として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[538年]]（[[宣化天皇]]3年）、勅命により創建された。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、[[1868年]]（明治初年）に高屋神社に改称し、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列格した。[[1904年]]（明治41年）5月11日、[[白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|白鳥神社]]（羽曳野市古市1-1）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも2018年現在も高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ==境内== === 境内社 === *末社：宮守稲荷社（祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市6-12 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]下車後、徒歩約15分（南へ約1.2km） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[辛国神社]] - 藤井寺市にある式内社。高屋神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である辛国連の本貫地であった。 {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Takaya Shrine'''|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

revision.comment: 16 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=高屋神社 |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[饒速日命]]
[[安閑天皇|広国押武金日命]] |社格=[[式内小社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=[[宣化天皇]]3年（[[538年]]） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高屋神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]と広国押武金日命（[[安閑天皇]]）を祀る。 当地は[[物部氏]]の系統で、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、[[祖神]]として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[538年]]（[[宣化天皇]]3年）、勅命により創建された。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、[[1868年]]（明治初年）に高屋神社に改称し、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列格した。[[1904年]]（明治41年）5月11日、[[白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|白鳥神社]]（羽曳野市古市1-1）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも2018年現在も高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ==境内== === 境内社 === *末社：宮守稲荷社（祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市6-12 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]下車後、徒歩約15分（南へ約1.2km） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[辛国神社]] - 藤井寺市にある式内社。高屋神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である辛国連の本貫地であった。 {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

[[安閑天皇|広国押武金日命]] |社格=[[式内小社]]

category|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:高屋神社 (羽曳野市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11669282}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|version=107260041|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=高屋神社 |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[饒速日命]]
[[安閑天皇|広国押武金日命]] |社格=[[式内小社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=[[宣化天皇]]3年（[[538年]]） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高屋神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]と広国押武金日命（[[安閑天皇]]）を祀る。 当地は[[物部氏]]の系統で、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、[[祖神]]として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[538年]]（[[宣化天皇]]3年）、勅命により創建された。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、[[1868年]]（明治初年）に高屋神社に改称し、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列格した。[[1904年]]（明治41年）5月11日、[[白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|白鳥神社]]（羽曳野市古市1-1）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも2018年現在も高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ==境内== === 境内社 === *末社：宮守稲荷社（祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市6-12 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]下車後、徒歩約15分（南へ約1.2km） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[辛国神社]] - 藤井寺市にある式内社。高屋神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である辛国連の本貫地であった。 {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

category|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:高屋神社 (羽曳野市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11669282}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|version=107260041|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=高屋神社 |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[饒速日命]]
[[安閑天皇|広国押武金日命]] |社格=[[式内小社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=[[宣化天皇]]3年（[[538年]]） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高屋神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]と広国押武金日命（[[安閑天皇]]）を祀る。 当地は[[物部氏]]の系統で、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、[[祖神]]として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[538年]]（[[宣化天皇]]3年）、勅命により創建された。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、[[1868年]]（明治初年）に高屋神社に改称し、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列格した。[[1904年]]（明治41年）5月11日、[[白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|白鳥神社]]（羽曳野市古市1-1）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも2018年現在も高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ==境内== === 境内社 === *末社：宮守稲荷社（祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市6-12 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]下車後、徒歩約15分（南へ約1.2km） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[辛国神社]] - 藤井寺市にある式内社。高屋神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である辛国連の本貫地であった。 {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{commons category|Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:高屋神社 (羽曳野市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11669282}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|version=107260041|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=高屋神社 |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[饒速日命]]
[[安閑天皇|広国押武金日命]] |社格=[[式内小社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=[[宣化天皇]]3年（[[538年]]） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高屋神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]と広国押武金日命（[[安閑天皇]]）を祀る。 当地は[[物部氏]]の系統で、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、[[祖神]]として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[538年]]（[[宣化天皇]]3年）、勅命により創建された。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、[[1868年]]（明治初年）に高屋神社に改称し、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列格した。[[1904年]]（明治41年）5月11日、[[白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|白鳥神社]]（羽曳野市古市1-1）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも2018年現在も高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ==境内== === 境内社 === *末社：宮守稲荷社（祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市6-12 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]下車後、徒歩約15分（南へ約1.2km） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[辛国神社]] - 藤井寺市にある式内社。高屋神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である辛国連の本貫地であった。 {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904}} ==References== [[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{commons category|Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:高屋神社 (羽曳野市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11669282}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|version=107260041|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=高屋神社 |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}古市 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|饒速日命}}
{{jalink|安閑天皇|Q241765|広国押武金日命}} |社格={{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}
旧{{jalink|村社}} |創建={{jalink|宣化天皇|Q329728}}3年（{{jalink|538年|Q32894}}） |本殿=一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高屋神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社}}。 ==祭神== {{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}と広国押武金日命（{{jalink|安閑天皇|Q241765}}）を祀る。 当地は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の系統で、『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、{{jalink|538年|Q32894}}（{{jalink|宣化天皇|Q329728}}3年）、勅命により創建された。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、{{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（明治初年）に高屋神社に改称し、{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）に村社に列格した。{{jalink|1904年|Q2046}}（明治41年）5月11日、{{jalink|白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|Q11580598|白鳥神社}}（羽曳野市古市1-1）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}された。{{jalink|1954年|Q18581}}（昭和29年）に高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも2018年現在も高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ==境内== === 境内社 === *末社：宮守稲荷社（祭神：{{jalink|宇迦之御魂大神}}） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}古市6-12 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：{{jalink|近鉄南大阪線|Q901342}}{{jalink|古市駅 (大阪府)|Q5509796|古市駅}}下車後、徒歩約15分（南へ約1.2km） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *{{jalink|辛国神社|Q48762570}} - 藤井寺市にある式内社。高屋神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である辛国連の本貫地であった。 {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建={{jalink|宣化天皇|Q329728}}3年（{{jalink|538年|Q32894}}） |本殿=一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高屋神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 ===祭神=== {{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}と広国押武金日命（{{jalink|安閑天皇|Q241765}}）を祀る。 当地は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の系統で、『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 ===歴史=== 社伝によれば、{{jalink|538年|Q32894}}（{{jalink|宣化天皇|Q329728}}3年）、勅命により創建された。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、{{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（明治初年）に高屋神社に改称し、{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）に村社に列格した。{{jalink|1904年|Q2046}}（明治41年）5月11日、{{jalink|白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|Q11580598|白鳥神社}}（羽曳野市古市1-1）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}された。{{jalink|1954年|Q18581}}（昭和29年）に高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも2018年現在も高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ===境内=== === 境内社 === *末社：宮守稲荷社（祭神：{{jalink|宇迦之御魂大神}}） === 現地情報 === ;所在地 *{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}古市6-12 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：{{jalink|近鉄南大阪線|Q901342}}{{jalink|古市駅 (大阪府)|Q5509796|古市駅}}下車後、徒歩約15分（南へ約1.2km） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} ===関連項目=== *{{jalink|辛国神社|Q48762570}} - 藤井寺市にある式内社。高屋神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である辛国連の本貫地であった。 {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建={{jalink|宣化天皇|Q329728}}3年（{{jalink|538年|Q32894}}） |本殿=一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高屋神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 ===祭神=== {{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}と広国押武金日命（{{jalink|安閑天皇|Q241765}}）を祀る。 当地は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の系統で、『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 ===歴史=== 社伝によれば、{{jalink|538年|Q32894}}（{{jalink|宣化天皇|Q329728}}3年）、勅命により創建された。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、{{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（明治初年）に高屋神社に改称し、{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）に村社に列格した。{{jalink|1904年|Q2046}}（明治41年）5月11日、{{jalink|白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|Q11580598|白鳥神社}}（羽曳野市古市1-1）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}された。{{jalink|1954年|Q18581}}（昭和29年）に高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも2018年現在も高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ===境内=== === 境内社 === *末社：宮守稲荷社（祭神：{{jalink|宇迦之御魂大神}}） === 現地情報 === ;所在地 *{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}古市6-12 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：{{jalink|近鉄南大阪線|Q901342}}{{jalink|古市駅 (大阪府)|Q5509796|古市駅}}下車後、徒歩約15分（南へ約1.2km） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} ===関連項目=== *{{jalink|辛国神社|Q48762570}} - 藤井寺市にある式内社。高屋神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である辛国連の本貫地であった。 {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建={{jalink|宣化天皇|Q329728}}3年（{{jalink|538年|Q32894}}） |本殿=一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=高屋神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''高屋神社'''（たかやじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 ===祭神=== {{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}と広国押武金日命（{{jalink|安閑天皇|Q241765}}）を祀る。 当地は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の系統で、『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「饒速日命の十世の孫、伊己止尼大連の後」と記される「高屋連」の本貫地であり、{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}として饒速日命を祀ったものである。広国押武金日命は後に祀られたものである。 ===歴史=== 社伝によれば、{{jalink|538年|Q32894}}（{{jalink|宣化天皇|Q329728}}3年）、勅命により創建された。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国古市郡 高屋神社」と記載され小社に列格しているが、{{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}には現れない。 江戸時代には八幡社と称していたが、{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（明治初年）に高屋神社に改称し、{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}（明治5年）に村社に列格した。{{jalink|1904年|Q2046}}（明治41年）5月11日、{{jalink|白鳥神社 (羽曳野市)|Q11580598|白鳥神社}}（羽曳野市古市1-1）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}された。{{jalink|1954年|Q18581}}（昭和29年）に高屋神社は白鳥神社から独立して旧社地に復興されたが、白鳥神社でも2018年現在も高屋神社の祭神が合祀神として祀られている。 ===境内=== === 境内社 === *末社：宮守稲荷社（祭神：{{jalink|宇迦之御魂大神}}） === 現地情報 === ;所在地 *{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}古市6-12 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：{{jalink|近鉄南大阪線|Q901342}}{{jalink|古市駅 (大阪府)|Q5509796|古市駅}}下車後、徒歩約15分（南へ約1.2km） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} ===関連項目=== *{{jalink|辛国神社|Q48762570}} - 藤井寺市にある式内社。高屋神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である辛国連の本貫地であった。 {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11669282}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takaya Shrine |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765|Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} 3 ({{jalink|538|Q32894}}) |本殿=One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}, and in ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{jalink|538|Q32894}} ({{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} 3). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|national histories}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{jalink|1904|Q2046}} (Meiji 41), it was {{jalink|merged|Q11590700}} into {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{jalink|1954|Q18581}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama Ōkami}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{jalink|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|Furuichi Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{jalink|Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

Torii and haiden |所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765|Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} 3 ({{jalink|538|Q32894}}) |本殿=One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}, and in ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{jalink|538|Q32894}} ({{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} 3). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|national histories}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{jalink|1904|Q2046}} (Meiji 41), it was {{jalink|merged|Q11590700}} into {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{jalink|1954|Q18581}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama Ōkami}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{jalink|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|Furuichi Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{jalink|Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11669282}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takaya Shrine |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765|Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} 3 ({{jalink|538|Q32894}}) |本殿=One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}, and in ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{jalink|538|Q32894}} ({{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} 3). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|national histories}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{jalink|1904|Q2046}} (Meiji 41), it was {{jalink|merged|Q11590700}} into {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{jalink|1954|Q18581}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama Ōkami}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{jalink|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|Furuichi Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{jalink|Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

Torii and haiden |所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765|Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} 3 ({{jalink|538|Q32894}}) |本殿=One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}, and in ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{jalink|538|Q32894}} ({{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} 3). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|national histories}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{jalink|1904|Q2046}} (Meiji 41), it was {{jalink|merged|Q11590700}} into {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{jalink|1954|Q18581}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama Ōkami}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{jalink|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|Furuichi Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{jalink|Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{ill||lt=Ukanomitama Ōkami|UNKNOWN}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{ill|lt=Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|WD=Q5509796|lt=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{ill|lt=Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570|WD=Q48762570}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|lt=Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}}) |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, and in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=national histories}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046}} (Meiji 41), it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700}} into {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|WD=Q11580598|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama Ōkami|UNKNOWN}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{ill|lt=Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|WD=Q5509796|lt=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{ill|lt=Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570|WD=Q48762570}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11669282}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takaya Shrine |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|lt=Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}}) |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, and in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=national histories}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046}} (Meiji 41), it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700}} into {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|WD=Q11580598|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{ill|lt=Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|WD=Q5509796|lt=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{ill|lt=Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570|WD=Q48762570}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|lt=Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}}) |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, and in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=national histories}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046}} (Meiji 41), it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700}} into {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|WD=Q11580598|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{ill|lt=Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|WD=Q5509796|lt=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{ill|lt=Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570|WD=Q48762570}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市古市', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:高屋神社 (羽曳野市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11669282}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takaya Shrine |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|lt=Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ankan}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}}) |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}} (Meiji 41), it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|WD=Q11580598|lt=Shiratori Shrine|1=Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama Ōkami|1=Ukanomitama}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{ill|lt=Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|WD=Q5509796|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{ill|lt=Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570|WD=Q48762570|1=Karakuni Shrine}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

|画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|lt=Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ankan}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}}) |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}} (Meiji 41), it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|WD=Q11580598|lt=Shiratori Shrine|1=Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama Ōkami|1=Ukanomitama}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{ill|lt=Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|WD=Q5509796|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{ill|lt=Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570|WD=Q48762570|1=Karakuni Shrine}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|lt=Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ankan}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}}) |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}} (Meiji 41), it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|WD=Q11580598|lt=Shiratori Shrine|1=Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama Ōkami|1=Ukanomitama}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{ill|lt=Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|WD=Q5509796|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{ill|lt=Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570|WD=Q48762570|1=Karakuni Shrine}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

* {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市古市', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:高屋神社 (羽曳野市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11669282}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takaya Shrine |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|lt=Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ankan}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}}) |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}} (Meiji 41), it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|WD=Q11580598|lt=Shiratori Shrine|1=Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama Ōkami|1=Ukanomitama}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{ill|lt=Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|WD=Q5509796|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{ill|lt=Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570|WD=Q48762570|1=Karakuni Shrine}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

* {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市古市', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[JA:高屋神社 (羽曳野市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11669282}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takaya Shrine |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|lt=Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ankan}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}}) |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}} (Meiji 41), it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|WD=Q11580598|lt=Shiratori Shrine|1=Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama Ōkami|1=Ukanomitama}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{ill|lt=Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|WD=Q5509796|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{ill|lt=Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570|WD=Q48762570|1=Karakuni Shrine}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]]

In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. |画像=[[File:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|lt=Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ankan}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}}) |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Takaya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaya Shrine''' (たかやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} and Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). This area belonged to the lineage of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' the “Takaya no Muraji” are recorded as descendants of Ikotone no Ōmuraji, 10th-generation descendant of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Thus Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined as ancestral deity. Hirokuni Oshitate Kanahi-no-Mikoto was enshrined later. ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order in {{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Takaya Shrine, Furuichi District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as small shrine, but it does not appear in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}. In the Edo period it was called Hachiman-sha, but in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (early Meiji) it was renamed Takaya Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked village shrine. On May 11, {{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}} (Meiji 41), it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Habikino)|Q11580598|WD=Q11580598|lt=Shiratori Shrine|1=Shiratori Shrine}} (Furuichi 1-1, Habikino). In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), Takaya Shrine separated from Shiratori Shrine and was restored at its former site, but as of 2018 the deities of Takaya Shrine are still enshrined at Shiratori Shrine as merged deities. ===Precinct=== === Precinct shrine === * Massha: Miyamori Inari-sha (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama Ōkami|1=Ukanomitama}}) === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Furuichi 6-12 ;Access * Nearest station: about 15 minutes on foot (about 1.2 km south) from {{ill|lt=Furuichi Station (Osaka)|Q5509796|WD=Q5509796|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|WD=Q901342|1=Minami Osaka Line}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== *{{ill|lt=Karakuni Shrine|Q48762570|WD=Q48762570|1=Karakuni Shrine}} - Shikinaisha in Fujiidera; like Takaya Shrine it enshrines Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and was the home territory of the Karakuni no Muraji, a branch of the Mononobe. {{commons|Category:Takaya-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]]
